<!-- Generated By: Claude Code (Claude Opus 4.7) -->

# why

Claude Code skill for deep 5-Whys investigations. Triggers on `/why`. Drills from a surface symptom or open question down to a root cause by alternating batched `AskUserQuestion` rounds (operator-side context) with parallel evidence-gathering from three specialised agents (global standards, local project docs, code + git history). Writes a durable `Why-<slug>.md` artifact to the project root.

License: Apache 2.0 (see `LICENSE`).

## Layout

```
SKILL.md                    # skill definition (loaded when /why fires)
agents/
  why-standards.md          # agent: ~/source/standards/ + global CLAUDE.md
  why-local-docs.md         # agent: project CLAUDE.md, README, ADRs, docs/
  why-code.md               # agent: code + git log/blame
templates/
  why-output.md             # output artifact template
```

## Install

The skill and the three agents live in different Claude Code directories, so they need separate symlinks (or a plugin wrapper). Pick whichever fits.

### Option 1 — user-level symlinks (personal use)

Clone or check out this repo somewhere stable, then symlink:

**Linux / macOS / WSL:**

```sh
REPO=~/source/claude-skill-why
mkdir -p ~/.claude/skills/why ~/.claude/agents
ln -s "$REPO/SKILL.md"                    ~/.claude/skills/why/SKILL.md
ln -s "$REPO/templates"                   ~/.claude/skills/why/templates
ln -s "$REPO/agents/why-standards.md"     ~/.claude/agents/why-standards.md
ln -s "$REPO/agents/why-local-docs.md"    ~/.claude/agents/why-local-docs.md
ln -s "$REPO/agents/why-code.md"          ~/.claude/agents/why-code.md
```

**Windows (PowerShell, run as Administrator or with Developer Mode enabled):**

```powershell
$repo = "$env:USERPROFILE\source\claude-skill-why"
New-Item -ItemType Directory -Force "$env:USERPROFILE\.claude\skills\why" | Out-Null
New-Item -ItemType Directory -Force "$env:USERPROFILE\.claude\agents"     | Out-Null
New-Item -ItemType SymbolicLink -Path "$env:USERPROFILE\.claude\skills\why\SKILL.md"       -Target "$repo\SKILL.md"
New-Item -ItemType SymbolicLink -Path "$env:USERPROFILE\.claude\skills\why\templates"      -Target "$repo\templates"
New-Item -ItemType SymbolicLink -Path "$env:USERPROFILE\.claude\agents\why-standards.md"   -Target "$repo\agents\why-standards.md"
New-Item -ItemType SymbolicLink -Path "$env:USERPROFILE\.claude\agents\why-local-docs.md"  -Target "$repo\agents\why-local-docs.md"
New-Item -ItemType SymbolicLink -Path "$env:USERPROFILE\.claude\agents\why-code.md"        -Target "$repo\agents\why-code.md"
```

### Option 2 — project-level

Same idea, but symlink into a specific project's `.claude/skills/` and `.claude/agents/` instead of the user home. Useful when you only want `/why` available inside one repo.

### Verify

After installing, start a Claude Code session and confirm:

- The `why` skill appears in the available skills list.
- `why-standards`, `why-local-docs`, and `why-code` appear as available subagent types (you should see them in the `Agent` tool's `subagent_type` enum).

If the agents are missing, the skill will refuse to dispatch correctly. Re-check the `~/.claude/agents/` symlinks.

## Usage

```
/why
/why <a refining question or scope hint>
```

- No argument: the skill takes the current problem from the conversation.
- With argument: that argument becomes (or refines) the seed question.

Run output: a `Why-<slug>.md` file in the current working directory, plus a short chat summary (seed question, root cause in one sentence, top next step).

## Uninstall

Remove the symlinks (or the plugin). The repo itself is unaffected.

```sh
rm ~/.claude/skills/why/SKILL.md ~/.claude/skills/why/templates
rmdir ~/.claude/skills/why
rm ~/.claude/agents/why-standards.md ~/.claude/agents/why-local-docs.md ~/.claude/agents/why-code.md
```

```powershell
Remove-Item "$env:USERPROFILE\.claude\skills\why\SKILL.md"
Remove-Item "$env:USERPROFILE\.claude\skills\why\templates"
Remove-Item "$env:USERPROFILE\.claude\skills\why" -Recurse
Remove-Item "$env:USERPROFILE\.claude\agents\why-standards.md"
Remove-Item "$env:USERPROFILE\.claude\agents\why-local-docs.md"
Remove-Item "$env:USERPROFILE\.claude\agents\why-code.md"
```
